name: Lens
summary: A magnifying lens component that uses CSS mask-image to create a zoomed circular region following the cursor.
detailedDescription: Lens creates a magnifying glass effect over any content using CSS radial gradient masks. The component tracks mouse position within its container and applies a circular mask (default 170px diameter) that reveals a zoomed version of the content underneath. The zoom is achieved by scaling the content (default 1.5x) with transformOrigin set to the mouse position. The mask uses radial-gradient with black at 100% transitioning to transparent, creating a sharp circular boundary. Framer Motion handles enter/exit animations with opacity and scale transitions (0.58 to 1, duration 0.3s). The component supports two modes: interactive mode follows cursor position, while static mode uses fixed x/y coordinates. Hover state management supports both internal state and external control via hovering/setHovering props. The container has overflow-hidden and rounded-lg styling. This creates an elegant magnification effect ideal for product detail views, image inspection, data visualization zoom, or any context where users need to examine content at higher magnification without losing context.
purpose: Provide magnified view of content under cursor
Create circular zoom lens following mouse
Enable detail inspection without losing context
behavior: Applies radial gradient mask at cursor position
Scales content with transform at mouse origin
Animates lens enter/exit with opacity and scale
Handles both internal and external hover state
visualCharacteristics: Sharp mask boundary with gradient edge
recommendations: Use where moderate motion and reusable composition improve UX and clarity.
